topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The attic air hung thick and dusty, smelling of aged paper and forgotten lavender. Liam was supposed to be looking for old photo albums, but his fingers brushed against a large, unmarked cardboard box tucked beneath a moth-eaten tapestry. Inside, nestled among yellowed tissue paper, were notebooks. Dozens of them. Every single assignment from elementary school, high school, even some college papers, meticulously cataloged and preserved. Liam sank onto an overturned crate, his breath catching in shallow, rapid gasps. His hands began to tremble so violently he had to grip the edge of the crate to steady them. "This... this isn't possible," he whispered, the sound thin and reedy in the silence. A strange, hot pressure built behind his eyes, blurring the edges of the room. He started pacing a tight circle, his movements jerky, unable to focus on anything but the sheer volume of preserved effort. He dropped to his knees, running a hand through his already messy hair, a choked sound escaping his throat as tears welled up, blurring the neat, perfect script of his mother's relentless documentation.

[story 2]

Sarah found the box behind the false panel in the linen closet. When she lifted the lid, a cascade of textbooks and meticulously organized folders spilled out. It was her mother’s life, documented in triplicate. Sarah felt a sudden, dizzying lurch in her stomach, a feeling like the floor tilting beneath her feet. She stared at a stack of essays from her freshman year, the handwriting impossibly neat. Her jaw tightened, muscles straining beneath her skin. "How... how did she do this?" she managed, her voice tight, almost a squeak. She began to shake her head, a frantic, jerky motion, trying to dislodge the reality of the situation. Her vision tunneled slightly at the periphery. She picked up a crumpled worksheet, and the paper seemed to vibrate under her touch. A low, ragged sound escaped her lips, and she pressed her palms hard against her temples, trying to physically contain the sudden, overwhelming surge of feeling that threatened to buckle her knees.

[story 3]

Mark stumbled upon the hidden compartment while cleaning out the basement. Inside, stacked floor to ceiling, were binders labeled with dates and subjects—every homework assignment his mother had ever completed, every test, every project. Mark stood frozen, his mouth slightly agape. The sheer volume was staggering, an overwhelming weight pressing down on his chest. He took a deep, shuddering breath that didn't quite reach his lungs. His hands started to flutter, unable to settle, moving restlessly as if trying to catch something intangible. "This is... too much," he murmured, his voice strained and high-pitched. He took a step back, bumping into a support beam, and his eyes darted frantically from one binder to the next, unable to process the organized chaos. A wave of intense agitation washed over him; he began pacing the narrow space, his steps quick and uneven, his whole body vibrating with an unmanageable tension that made his skin prickle.
